Title: RING-Type E3 Ligase OsRFPH2-6 Ubiquitinates Nuclear Factor Y Subunit OsNF-YA1 to Suppress Rice Immunity

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RING-H2 type E3 ubiquitin ligase; C-terminal RING domain; N-terminal transmembrane motif
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving rice disease resistance by loss-of-function editing or reducing expression; can be combined with editing of ONAC083-OsRFPH2-6-OsNF-YA1 module; caution because study addresses immunity rather than nutrient-quality traits directly
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in rice; prior CRISPR/Cas9 knockout evidence cited; in vitro E3 ligase assay; cysteine mutagenesis; Y2H; LCI; GST pull-down; BiFC; in vitro ubiquitination; in planta and cell-free degradation assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae strain RB22 infection; chitin treatment; MG132 in degradation assays
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoalexins and phenylpropanoids are defense-associated metabolites contributing to rice immunity
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported as induced by M. oryzae infection and transcriptionally activated by ONAC083
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed transgenic lines showed strong transcript accumulation; endogenous gene implicated during pathogen challenge
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates rice blast resistance and chitin-triggered immunity by ubiquitinating OsNF-YA1 and promoting its degradation, thereby suppressing defense-associated transcription including metabolite-pathway genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsRFPH2-6 overexpression increased lesion size and fungal growth in rice after M. oryzae infection. 2. OsRFPH2-6 possesses RING-dependent E3 ubiquitin ligase activity requiring conserved cysteines. 3. OsRFPH2-6 interacts with OsNF-YA1, ubiquitinates it, and promotes its proteasomal degradation. 4. Through OsNF-YA1 degradation, OsRFPH2-6 suppresses defense genes including genes for phytoalexin and phenylpropanoid pathways.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter or constitutive transgene expression context not explicitly stated; overexpression construct in pCAMBIA-1300s-GFP
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core phenotype is immune regulation; metabolite connection is indirect through transcriptional control of phytoalexin/phenylpropanoid biosynthetic genes. Article is not a primary nutrient-quality paper.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Primarily nucleus for interaction with OsNF-YA1 in BiFC; individual proteins observed throughout cells in transient assays

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NF-YA subunit of Nuclear Factor Y transcription factor complex
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive target for engineering disease resistance by stabilizing or enhancing expression/activity; may be combined with disruption of OsRFPH2-6; caution because OsNF-YA1 has reported opposite role in antiviral immunity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in rice; blast inoculation; ROS burst assay; OsWRKY45 expression assay; MAPK activation assay; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; interaction and degradation assays with OsRFPH2-6
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May have pathogen-specific opposing roles; reported negative role in rice antiviral immunity in prior literature
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae strain RB22 infection; chitin treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoalexins and phenylpropanoids contribute to defense against blast disease
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional analyses performed in leaves of 3- to 4-week-old plants; protein interaction observed in nucleus
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates rice blast resistance and chitin-triggered immunity; knockout downregulates defense-associated metabolic genes including diterpenoid phytoalexin and phenylpropanoid pathway genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR knockout of OsNF-YA1 increased blast susceptibility and reduced chitin-triggered ROS, OsWRKY45 induction, and MAPK activation. 2. RNA-seq showed that osnf-ya1 downregulated diterpenoid biosynthesis and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis pathways. 3. Positive defense/metabolism genes including OsCPS4, CYP76M6, OsDXS3, OsPAL6 and Os4CL1 were reduced in osnf-ya1. 4. OsNF-YA1 is ubiquitinated and degraded by OsRFPH2-6.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsRFPH2-6; putative NF-YB/NF-YC subunits not resolved in this paper
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter in CRISPR/Cas9 vector context for mutant generation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Metabolite regulation is indirect and inferred from transcriptome pathway enrichment and expression of biosynthetic genes; direct target genes of OsNF-YA1 were not established.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus for interaction; also observed throughout cells in transient localization assays

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Prior work cited established ONAC083 as an upstream activator of OsRFPH2-6. 2. Current paper integrates ONAC083 into the OsRFPH2-6-OsNF-YA1 module. 3. ONAC083 expression was upregulated in osnf-ya1 and is associated with negative regulation of rice immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally characterized in the present Results as a focal experimental gene; included because the paper frames a regulatory module, but evidence is partly from previous studies.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of rice immunity; activates OsRFPH2-6 expression, which indirectly suppresses defense-associated metabolic genes via OsNF-YA1 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior studies; current study provides supporting transcript evidence from osnf-ya1 RNA-seq/RT-qPCR context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of ONAC083-OsRFPH2-6-OsNF-YA1 regulatory module; feedback not established
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1314397 (expression changes in osnf-ya1 context)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated upon M. oryzae infection in prior work; upregulated in osnf-ya1 plants in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function editing may reduce OsRFPH2-6 expression and enhance resistance; best considered as part of the regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsRFPH2-6 transcription by binding the ACGCAA cis-element in its promoter

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNF-YA1 is a positive regulator of blast resistance and chitin-triggered immunity. 2. Loss of OsNF-YA1 reprograms transcription, with downregulation of diterpenoid phytoalexin and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis genes. 3. OsNF-YA1 likely promotes defense metabolism-associated transcription, but direct targets were not established. 4. OsRFPH2-6 ubiquitinates and degrades OsNF-YA1.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation as NF-YA subunit; positive regulation of defense-associated genes; activity suppressed by ubiquitin-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection/chitin-triggered immunity; OsRFPH2-6-mediated ubiquitination; likely NF-Y complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct DNA targets not demonstrated; likely functions in NF-Y trimer complexes with NF-YB/NF-YC. Also reported in prior literature to negatively regulate antiviral immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes expression of defense-associated metabolic genes including phytoalexin and phenylpropanoid pathway genes, supporting blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; pathogen assay; ROS and MAPK assays; Y2H; LCI; pull-down; BiFC; ubiquitination and degradation assays
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsCPS4; CYP76M6; OsDXS3; OsPAL6; Os4CL1; OsATL15; OsWRKY6; OsPR10a; chi11 (supported as expression-associated targets, not proven direct)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of defense-associated biosynthesis and immune response genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed in rice leaves; interaction signal localized mainly in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pathogen-dependent functional divergence; prior negative role in antiviral immunity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator of defense metabolic gene expression supporting phytoalexin/phenylpropanoid-associated immunity
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; also whole-cell distribution observed in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be stabilized or enhanced to improve blast resistance, potentially together with OsRFPH2-6 disruption; caution about antiviral tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as NF-Y trimer subunit with NF-YB and NF-YC, though specific partners were not identified here
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with expression of OsCPS4, CYP76M6, OsDXS3, OsPAL6, Os4CL1, OsATL15, OsWRKY6, OsPR10a and chi11; negatively associated with ONAC083, MNAC3 and OsSGR
